summary: The frontend container follows a component‑based Angular architecture, organized into presentation, service, and adapter/integration layers and leveraging TypeScript, RxJS, Angular Material, and Angular CLI for build and runtime. It implements the core deed‑registry domain concepts (Deed, Document, Workflow, User, etc.) and provides rich capabilities such as deed creation, PDF handling, workflow orchestration, authentication, help, rate limiting, and reporting through a suite of REST‑driven adapters. Quality metrics show good separation of concerns but moderate coupling and cohesion, with a high count of services, components, and pipes that signal architectural debt and the need for tighter modularization and documentation.

layers: Presentation Layer (Components, Directives, Pipes, Guards, Interceptors)
technologies: NGXS/Redux (if used, not visible but common)
technical.integration_style: REST API (HTTP GET/POST endpoints defined in interfaces)

capabilities: Create, edit, view and approve Deeds
Import and export Deed data (JSON, bulk capture)
Manage Deed lifecycle status and correction notes
Handle document metadata, PDF generation and archiving
Execute and monitor workflow tasks, jobs and reencryption processes
Perform user authentication, session management and permission checks
Provide online help and contextual assistance
Apply rate limiting, error handling and busy‑indicator UI feedback
Generate and sign operation tokens for secure actions
Produce reports and metadata for regulatory compliance
functional.bounded_context: Deed Registry and Workflow Management (Document & Signature Processing)
integrations: Backend REST APIs via Xnp endpoints (DeedRegistryApi, DocumentMetadataApi, JobApi, TaskApi, ReencryptionApi, ActionApi, ReportMetadataApi)
Session and JSON import adapters (NativeSessionAdapter, NativeJsonImportAdapter)

debt_indicators: Excessive number of services (131) indicating potential God‑service syndrome
Very high component count (128) suggesting bloated UI layer
Large number of pipes (67) that may overlap in functionality
Sparse explicit relation mapping – unclear dependency graph
Potential missing domain‑level abstractions in adapter and service layers
recommendations: Refactor oversized services into smaller, focused services or feature‑specific service groups
Introduce well‑defined interfaces or abstract base classes to decouple adapters from concrete implementations
Consolidate similar pipes into shared utility functions or fewer, more expressive pipes
Enforce stricter module boundaries (e.g., feature modules per bounded context) to reduce cross‑cutting dependencies
Add architectural documentation and linting rules to monitor component/service count growth

overall_assessment: The frontend exhibits a solid Angular foundation and covers the required business capabilities, but its growing number of services, components, and pipes is creating maintainability risk. Moderate coupling and cohesion combined with the identified debt suggest a need for systematic refactoring and stricter module boundaries to sustain scalability and developer productivity.
top_recommendations: Refactor oversized services into smaller, focused services or feature‑specific groups
Introduce well‑defined interfaces or abstract base classes to decouple adapters
Enforce stricter module boundaries per bounded context to limit cross‑cutting dependencies

layers: Presentation Layer (Components, Directives, Pipes, Guards, Interceptors)
